summary: Honour --follow=false in tail and fix the hint list's hyphenated names to match Etherpad's camelCase API paths.
reason: Greptile P1 (two findings) on PR #396: (1) the `follow` flag is declared, registered, and documented ("set --follow=false for single poll") but the variable is never checked inside RunE — the ticker for-loop runs indefinitely regardless. (2) `tailKnownResources()` returned hyphenated Cobra command names (`"get-stats"`, `"list-all-pads"`, ...) but tail builds the HTTP path as `"/" + resource` and Etherpad's API endpoints are camelCase (`/getStats`, `/listAllPads`, ...). Every hint was guaranteed to 404 on a real Etherpad instance.
validated_outcome: RunE now returns after the initial fetch when follow == false (banner message also branches between "Tailing ..." and "Polling ... once"). tailKnownResources() now uses camelCase API path segments matching each promoted command's `pp:path` annotation. The JSON help envelope's `note` field flags the additional constraint that most Etherpad endpoints take query parameters tail's bare GET doesn't pass — the no-param endpoints that work as-is are listAllPads, listAllGroups, and getStats.
upstream_issue: TODO: file on mvanhorn/cli-printing-press — tail template should derive the hint list from the OpenAPI path segments (not the kebab-cased CLI command names) and should always wire its --follow flag into the loop condition.
